feature: P1: Set up Sentry error tracking dashboard
workflow_rationale: This task adds new infrastructure capabilities (hardened SDK config, typed helpers, alerting docs, dashboards) to an existing Sentry SDK installation following a progressive enhancement pattern. Single-service (Next.js) with 8 phases across config files, lib split, error boundary updates, env vars, example route, and documentation.

phases.name: Server SDK Config
phases.description: Update sentry.server.config.ts with environment-differentiated tracesSampleRate, spotlight for dev tracing UI, WebVitals attachment via beforeSendTransaction, and registerSpanErrorInstrumentation call.

subtasks.description: Update sentry.server.config.ts with env-differentiated sampling (1.0 dev / 0.05 prod), spotlight: isDev, beforeSendTransaction callback for WebVitals, and Sentry.registerSpanErrorInstrumentation() call

verification.command: grep -c "NODE_ENV.*development" sentry.server.config.ts && grep -c "spotlight" sentry.server.config.ts && grep -c "registerSpanErrorInstrumentation" sentry.server.config.ts

subtasks.notes: Verified: grep counts for NODE_ENV.*development, spotlight, and registerSpanErrorInstrumentation all returned 1. Commit 6203929 created.

phases.name: Client SDK Config
phases.description: Update sentry.client.config.ts with environment-differentiated tracesSampleRate, replay sample rates, feedback integration with autoInject:false, WebVitals attachment, and conditional debug flag.

subtasks.description: Update sentry.client.config.ts with env-differentiated sampling (1.0 dev / 0.05 prod), replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.1, replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0, feedbackIntegration with autoInject:false, and conditional debug

verification.command: grep -c "NODE_ENV.*development" sentry.client.config.ts && grep -c "feedbackIntegration" sentry.client.config.ts && grep -c "autoInject.*false" sentry.client.config.ts

subtasks.notes: Verified: grep counts for NODE_ENV.*development, feedbackIntegration, and autoInject.*false all returned 1. Commit 95e8dee created.

phases.name: Edge SDK Config
phases.description: Update sentry.edge.config.ts with environment-differentiated tracesSampleRate matching the server pattern.

subtasks.description: Update sentry.edge.config.ts with env-differentiated sampling (1.0 dev / 0.05 prod) and conditional debug flag
subtasks.notes: Verified: grep count for NODE_ENV.*development returned 1. Commit 4d8b155 created.

phases.name: Sentry Lib Split
phases.description: Create lib/sentry/client.ts and lib/sentry/server.ts with typed utility exports. Update existing lib/sentry.ts to re-export from the new files for backward compatibility.

subtasks.description: Create lib/sentry/client.ts with typed exports: captureError, addBreadcrumb, setUser, setTag, setContext. Use Sentry SDK's captureError alias, setUser, setTag, setContext. Must be client-safe (no server-only APIs).
subtasks.notes: Verified: All 5 exports (captureError, addBreadcrumb, setUser, setTag, setContext) present. Commit 1d17cd5 created.

subtasks.description: Create lib/sentry/server.ts with typed server-side exports: captureError, addBreadcrumb, setUser, setTag, setContext, startSpan. Use server-only Sentry APIs.
subtasks.notes: Verified: All 6 exports (captureError, addBreadcrumb, setUser, setTag, setContext, startSpan) present. Commit de691f2 created.

subtasks.description: Update lib/sentry.ts to re-export from the new split files (lib/sentry/client.ts and lib/sentry/server.ts) for backward compatibility. Existing code importing from @/lib/sentry should continue working.

verification.command: grep -c 're-export' lib/sentry.ts && tsc --noEmit lib/sentry.ts 2>&1 | grep -c 'error' || true

subtasks.notes: Verified: grep count for 're-export' returned 1, tsc --noEmit returned 0 errors. Commit b56280c created.

phases.name: Instrumentation Hook
phases.description: Create instrumentation.ts at project root to register server-side callbacks. Must NOT call Sentry.init() (handled by sentry.server.config.ts). Use beforeSendTransaction pattern for WebVitals. Must NOT use registerSpanAttributesModifier (does not exist in SDK v8).

subtasks.description: Create instrumentation.ts at project root using Option B (Next.js 14.x pattern): conditionally import sentry.server.config based on NEXT_RUNTIME. Register beforeSendTransaction callback for WebVitals attachment. NO Sentry.init() call. Must NOT use registerSpanAttributesModifier.

verification.command: grep -c "NEXT_RUNTIME" instrumentation.ts && grep -c "Sentry.init" instrumentation.ts || echo "Sentry.init not found (correct)"

subtasks.notes: Verified: instrumentation.ts created with Option B pattern - conditional import of sentry.server.config for nodejs and sentry.edge.config for edge. NO Sentry.init() call. NEXT_RUNTIME appears in both conditional branches (required for both runtimes). Commit 3745400 created.

phases.name: Error Boundary Updates
phases.description: Update app/global-error.tsx and app/error.tsx to use new lib/sentry/client.ts helpers instead of the old lib/sentry utilities. Switch captureException to captureError from client helper.

subtasks.description: Update app/global-error.tsx to import captureError from @/lib/sentry/client instead of captureException from @/lib/sentry. Preserve existing UI, pass error.digest as tag, call captureError in useEffect.
subtasks.notes: Updated import from captureException @/lib/sentry to captureError @/lib/sentry/client. Removed console.error debugging statement. Verification passed (1 match for lib/sentry/client).

subtasks.description: Update app/error.tsx to import captureError from @/lib/sentry/client instead of captureException from @/lib/sentry. Preserve existing UI, pass error.digest as tag, call captureError in useEffect.
subtasks.notes: Updated import from captureException @/lib/sentry to captureError @/lib/sentry/client. Verification passed (1 match for lib/sentry/client). Commit 9eeb83b created.

phases.name: Next.js Config & Env Vars
phases.description: Update next.config.mjs sentryConfig with authToken, add SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT to .env.example, and add instrumentationHook experimental flag for Next.js 14.x.

subtasks.description: Update next.config.mjs sentryConfig object to add authToken: process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN. SENTRY_ORG and SENTRY_PROJECT already present. Keep existing tunnelRoute and hideSourceMaps.
subtasks.notes: Added authToken: process.env.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN to sentryConfig object. Verification passed (grep count = 1). Commit 3b9f78d created.

subtasks.description: Update .env.example with SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT, add descriptive comment to NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN, ensure SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN, SENTRY_ORG, SENTRY_PROJECT entries are present and well-documented.
Add experimental.instrumentationHook: true to next.config.mjs to enable instrumentation.ts for Next.js 14.x. Add under experimental key alongside existing config.
subtasks.notes: Added experimental.instrumentationHook: true to next.config.mjs under experimental key. Verification passed (grep count = 1). Commit 6f5b2ff created.

phases.name: Example Route & Documentation
phases.description: Create app/api/sentry-example-api/route.ts demonstrating Sentry.startSpan usage in App Router. Create sentry-alerting.md and sentry-dashboards.md with configuration documentation.

subtasks.description: Create app/api/sentry-example-api/route.ts demonstrating Sentry.startSpan({ name, op: 'http.server' }) pattern in App Router route handler. Include GET handler with named span wrapping full handler body.
subtasks.notes: Created app/api/sentry-example-api/route.ts with Sentry.startSpan wrapping GET handler (op: 'http.server'). File includes span attributes, error handling with captureException, and follows existing app/api/jobs/route.ts patterns. Verification passed: grep found Sentry.startSpan and http.server patterns. Commit 286d303 created.

subtasks.description: Create sentry-alerting.md documenting P0-P3 severity tier definitions, alert threshold SLIs (p95 latency, error rate %), notification channel routing (Slack #alerts-sentry, Discord server), ownership rules (path: and tag: syntax), and SLA mapping. Placeholders for Slack/Discord channel IDs (TBD from stakeholders).

verification.instructions: Review sentry-alerting.md: verify P0/P1/P2/P3 severity definitions exist, alert threshold SLI values are documented, notification routing (Slack/Discord) is defined with TBD placeholders for channel IDs, ownership rules use path:/tag: syntax

subtasks.notes: Created docs/sentry-alerting.md (493 lines) with: P0-P3 severity definitions, SLI thresholds (p95 latency, error rate %), Slack #alerts-sentry and Discord routing with TBD channel ID placeholders, path:/tag: ownership rules, and SLA mapping. Committed as b375f2a.

subtasks.description: Create sentry-dashboards.md documenting three dashboard templates: (1) Engineering Lead - overall health, error trends, team performance; (2) On-Call - active issues, P0/P1 alerts, deployment correlation; (3) Developer - personal issues, assigned items, velocity. Include widget specs (chart types, queries, refresh rates).

verification.instructions: Review sentry-dashboards.md: verify all three dashboard templates (Engineering Lead, On-Call, Developer) exist with widget specifications including chart types, queries, and refresh rates. Note that actual Sentry UI creation requires manual action at sentry.io.

subtasks.notes: Created docs/sentry-dashboards.md (759 lines) with: 3 dashboard templates (Engineering Lead, On-Call, Developer), 23 widget specifications each with chart type, query/filter, time range, interval, and placement. Includes tag requirements table, Sentry UI step-by-step instructions, and dashboard layout diagrams. Committed as a9d6f21.

parallel_groups.reason: All depend only on baseline (no prior phases), each modifies disjoint file sets
Both depend only on phase-4 (lib split), different file sets

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Parallel groups give ~2x speedup vs sequential. Phase 1 (server config) runs first alone as baseline.

acceptance_criteria: All existing vitest tests pass (pnpm test:run)
No TypeScript compilation errors (pnpm run type-check)
Sentry SDK initializes without crashing in dev mode
tracesSampleRate is 1.0 in dev (NODE_ENV=development), 0.05 in production
No SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN or other secrets in source code (grep scan)
Next.js build succeeds with Sentry config applied

verification_steps.name: Next.js Build
verification_steps.command: cd /Users/yanuar/Documents/daws/daily-worker-hub-web && NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=https://test@o0.ingest.sentry.io/0 SENTRY_ORG=test SENTRY_PROJECT=test SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=test pnpm run build 2>&1 | tail -20
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Build succeeds with Sentry config applied

verification_steps.name: Secrets Scan
verification_steps.command: grep -r 'sntrys_' --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' --include='*.js' --include='*.mjs' . | grep -v node_modules | grep -v .next | grep -v .auto-claude || echo 'No secrets found'
verification_steps.expected_outcome: No hardcoded secrets in source files

verification_steps.name: Sentry Config Verification
verification_steps.command: grep -c "tracesSampleRate.*isDev.*1.0" sentry.server.config.ts sentry.client.config.ts sentry.edge.config.ts | awk -F: '{sum+=$2} END{print sum}'

verification_strategy.reasoning: High risk - Sentry SDK integration touches error handling paths and session replay (user data). Security scan needed to verify SENTRY_DSN is not leaked and session replay privacy settings (maskAllText, blockAllMedia) are appropriate. Unit tests for custom sampling logic, type check for typed utility exports.
